S1.name: Closed Guard Hip Bump Sweep to Mount Armbar

identity.objective: Break posture, sweep to mount, finish with armbar.
identity.success: Arrive in mount with control. Armbar is the natural finish.
identity.failure: Sweep fails — still in closed guard. Mount lost — fall to half guard. Both are safe.

constraint.task: Hold closed guard for 2 minutes. Partner tries to posture up and open your guard.
constraint.discovery: Students discover: legs control hips, angle matters, grip breaks posture.

isolation.task: Control posture using only legs and hips. No hands.
isolation.discovery: Hip angle and leg pressure are the real posture-breaking mechanism. Hands assist but don't lead.

tool_add.effect: Pull head down. Feel posture collapse. Recognize the moment of instability.
Sit up explosively, post hand behind you, drive opponent over your shoulder.
Settle weight. Grapevines or heels on hips. No rush to submit.

resistance.level_1: Partner allows sweep, holds mount passively.
resistance.level_2: Partner postures at realistic speed. Does not resist sweep once off-balanced.
resistance.level_3: Partner defends posture actively. Resists sweep attempt. Tries to open guard.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Partner attempts guard breaks, posture recovery, mount escapes.

specific_sparring.reset: On guard open or sweep fail

S1.live: Free roll. Background objective: find closed guard → execute S1. Do not force it.

tasks: Maintain posture in closed guard. Hands on hips, head up, spine straight.
Defend the hip bump. Base wide, post hand when they sit up.
Escape mount. Bridge and roll, or elbow-knee escape.
Defend the armbar. Stack, posture up, pull arm free.

S2.name: Closed Guard Arm Drag to Back Control RNC

identity.objective: Arm drag to expose the back, climb to back control, finish RNC.
identity.success: Seatbelt grip with hooks, choke locked.
identity.failure: Drag fails — still in closed guard. Back lost — land in mount or guard. Both safe.

constraint.task: From closed guard, get to your partner's back without any specific technique instruction. 2 minutes.
constraint.discovery: Students discover: they need to break alignment, create an angle, get chest-to-back.

isolation.task: Control the back using only seatbelt grip and chest pressure. No hooks.
isolation.discovery: Chest-to-back connection is the primary control. Hooks are retention, not control.

tool_add.effect: Pull arm across, hip out, angle changes. Opponent's back is exposed.
Over-arm controls choking lane. Under-arm prevents turn-in. Hands clasped.
Bottom hook first. Top hook second. Heels dig inward.

resistance.level_1: Partner allows drag, stays still on back.
resistance.level_2: Partner moves at realistic speed. Does not defend the drag.
resistance.level_3: Partner defends the drag. Tries to turn in when back is taken.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Partner defends drag, fights seatbelt, attempts back escape.

S2.live: Free roll. Objective: find closed guard → arm drag → back → RNC.

tasks: Maintain posture in closed guard. Deny the 2-on-1.
When dragged: turn in immediately, face your partner, recover guard.
Defend back control: fight seatbelt, clear bottom hook, turn to face.
Defend RNC: protect neck, two hands on choking arm, create space to turn.

S3.name: Half Guard Pass to Side Control Americana

identity.objective: Pass half guard with crossface pressure, stabilize side control, finish americana.
identity.success: Clean pass to chest-on-chest side control. Americana from dominant position.
identity.failure: Pass fails — still in half guard (safe). Side control lost — fall to half guard (safe).

constraint.task: From top half guard, free your trapped leg. 2 minutes. No specific technique.
constraint.discovery: Shoulder pressure controls the head. Head control determines whether they can re-guard.

isolation.task: Top player: pin with only crossface and chest pressure. No hands on the mat.
isolation.discovery: Weight distribution is the invisible control layer. Heavy chest, light hands.

tool_add.effect: Shoulder blade drives into far cheek. Head turns away. Re-guard denied.
Arm under their armpit, hand on shoulder blade. Controls their hip movement.
Shin slides across their thigh. Hip drops to clear. Land in side control.

resistance.level_1: Bottom partner holds half guard loosely. Allows the pass.
resistance.level_2: Bottom partner holds half guard at realistic tension. Does not re-guard after pass.
resistance.level_3: Bottom partner actively fights for underhook, tries to re-guard.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Bottom partner attempts sweeps, back takes, re-guard.

S3.live: Free roll. Objective: find top half guard → pass → side control → americana.

tasks: Fight for the underhook. Whoever wins the underhook controls the position.
Deny the crossface. Frame on their bicep and shoulder.
When passed: immediate hip escape to re-guard. Do not stay flat.
Defend americana: keep elbow tight to body, bridge toward the trapped arm.

S4.name: Mount Escape to Butterfly Sweep

identity.objective: Escape mount, recover to butterfly guard, execute sweep to top.
identity.success: Clean mount escape. Butterfly hooks established. Sweep to top position.
identity.failure: Escape fails — still under mount (recoverable with bridge/shrimp cycle).

constraint.task: Survive mount for 2 minutes. Partner maintains but does not attack. Find ways to create space.
constraint.discovery: Flat on back = stuck. Frames create distance. Bridge creates movement opportunities.

isolation.task: Top player: hold mount using only hips and legs. Bottom player: escape using only hips.
isolation.discovery: Mount control is hip weight and base. Mount escape is hip movement and angle change.

tool_add.effect: Elbow connects to knee. Creates a wedge. Slide knee through to half guard.
Once space exists, insert instep hooks inside their thighs.
Overhook or underhook. Load their weight forward. Elevate with hooks. Sweep.

resistance.level_1: Top partner stays still in mount. Allows escape.
resistance.level_2: Top partner maintains mount at realistic weight. Does not re-mount after escape.
resistance.level_3: Top partner actively re-mounts, adjusts base, blocks escape attempts.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Top attacks while maintaining. Bottom must escape and sweep.

S4.live: Free roll. Objective: when mounted, execute escape → butterfly → sweep.

tasks: Maintain mount. Heavy hips. Grapevines or heels.
When they bridge: ride it, post, re-settle.
When they shrimp: follow their hips, deny space.
Recognize when butterfly hooks are inserted — kill the hooks before the sweep.

S5.name: Open Guard Collar-Sleeve Sweep to Mount Cross Choke

identity.objective: Control distance with grips, sweep to mount, finish with cross choke.
identity.success: Grip control → sweep → mount → cross choke.
identity.failure: Sweep fails — still in open guard (safe). Grips stripped — re-grip cycle.

constraint.task: Maintain open guard grips for 2 minutes. Partner tries to pass. Keep them at distance.
constraint.discovery: Feet on hips/biceps are distance managers. Grips are steering wheels.

isolation.task: Control partner using only feet on hips and shins on biceps. No hands.
isolation.discovery: Legs are the primary guard retention tool. Hands add precision but legs do the work.

tool_add.effect: Deep four-finger grip. Pull down for posture break. Cross-grip for choke setup.
Control their posting hand. Deny base on one side. Sweep becomes available.
Load weight to one side, kick legs in opposite direction, arrive in mount.

resistance.level_1: Partner stands still. Allows grips and sweep.
resistance.level_2: Partner moves at realistic speed. Allows sweep once off-balanced.
resistance.level_3: Partner strips grips actively. Attempts passes.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Partner passes, strips grips, defends sweeps.

S5.live: Free roll. Objective: pull to open guard → establish grips → sweep → mount → choke.

tasks: Strip collar grip immediately — two-on-one break.
Kill the sleeve grip before they load the sweep.
When swept: post hand, base wide, deny the mount.
Defend cross choke: posture in mount, hands inside, deny deep collar access.

S6.name: Side Control Escape to Closed Guard

identity.objective: Survive side control, create frames, hip escape to recover closed guard.
identity.success: Guard recovered. Opponent is back inside your guard.
identity.failure: Escape fails — still under side control (recoverable with frame + shrimp cycle).

constraint.task: Survive under side control for 2 minutes. Partner maintains heavy pressure. Find breathing space.
constraint.discovery: Flat = crushed. Frames on neck and hip create survival space. Getting to your side is critical.

isolation.task: Escape side control using only hip movement and shoulder turns. No arms.
isolation.discovery: The hip escape is the engine. Arms add frames but hips create the actual distance.

tool_add.effect: Forearm on their neck, elbow on their hip. Creates a cage they cannot collapse.
Bridge onto shoulder → slide hips away → create knee space.
Slide knee into the space. Reconnect guard. Close guard immediately.

resistance.level_1: Top partner holds side control loosely. Allows escape.
resistance.level_2: Top partner holds at realistic pressure. Does not re-pass after escape.
resistance.level_3: Top partner adjusts, re-passes, blocks hip escapes actively.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Top attacks while maintaining. Bottom must escape under pressure.

S6.live: Free roll. Objective: when stuck in side control, execute frame → escape → guard recovery.

tasks: Maintain side control. Crossface + underhook. Chest pressure heavy.
When they frame: swim through the frame, re-establish chest contact.
When they hip escape: follow their hips, deny the knee insertion.
Recognize when guard is about to close — pass or transition before it locks.

S7.name: Closed Guard Triangle Choke

identity.objective: Control posture, isolate one arm in and one arm out, lock triangle, cut the angle, finish.
identity.success: Triangle locked. Angle cut to 90°. Opponent taps.
identity.failure: Triangle not locked — still in closed guard (safe). Stacked — release and re-attack (safe).

constraint.task: From closed guard, try to get your legs around partner's head and one arm. 2 minutes. No technique instruction.
constraint.discovery: One arm in, one arm out is the prerequisite. Posture break creates the opening.

isolation.task: Lock the triangle position (legs around head+arm) without finishing. Hold it. Feel the shape.
isolation.discovery: The lock itself is a position. It must be stable before the finish is possible.

tool_add.effect: Control one arm with overhook. Push the other arm across with same-side hand.
Shin across back of neck. Lock ankle behind knee. Squeeze knees together.
Walk shoulders perpendicular to opponent. Pull head down. This is what finishes the choke.

resistance.level_1: Partner allows posture break and arm isolation. Stays in triangle.
resistance.level_2: Partner postures at realistic speed. Does not defend once triangle is locked.
resistance.level_3: Partner postures up, stacks, defends arm isolation.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Partner defends posture, stacks, attempts to pass out of triangle.

specific_sparring.win: Full road: posture break → arm isolation → triangle finish

S7.live: Free roll. Objective: find closed guard → hunt for the triangle.

tasks: Maintain posture. Hands on hips. Do not let head get pulled down.
When arm is isolated: pull it back immediately. Two-on-one to recover.
When triangle is locked: posture up, stack, walk around to pass side.
Defend the angle cut: drive forward, deny the perpendicular hip walk.

S8.name: Mount Cross Choke with Americana Trap

identity.objective: Stabilize mount, attack cross choke. When they defend the choke, americana is exposed.
identity.success: Cross choke finish, or americana finish when they defend the choke.
identity.failure: Both attacks defended — still in mount (safe). Mount lost — fall to half guard (safe).

constraint.task: Maintain mount for 3 minutes. Partner tries to escape. No attacks — just maintain.
constraint.discovery: Mount maintenance IS the technique. Without stable mount, no submission works.

isolation.task: Ride mount for 3 minutes against active escapes. Grapevines, base adjustments, hip pressure only.
isolation.discovery: Heavy hips and base adjustments are the foundation. Rushing submissions = mount loss.

tool_add.effect: Deep grip, four fingers inside collar. Elbow tight to chest.
Other hand mirrors first grip. Elbows drop. Forearms close around the neck.
When they push on your chest to defend: isolate the pushing arm. Figure-four. Paint brush to the mat.

resistance.level_1: Bottom partner stays still. Allows grips and choke.
resistance.level_2: Bottom partner escapes passively. Defends choke weakly.
resistance.level_3: Bottom partner actively defends collar grips. Attempts mount escape.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Bottom partner bridges, shrimps, strips grips, escapes.

specific_sparring.win: Maintain mount for 2 minutes, then attack

S8.live: Free roll. Objective: find mount → stabilize → cross choke → americana trap.

tasks: Protect collar. Deny deep grips. Two hands strip immediately.
When choke is sinking: turn to side, create frame, begin escape.
Keep elbows tight to deny americana setup.
Bridge and roll when they commit both hands to choke — their base is compromised.

S9.name: Collar-Sleeve Guard Pull to Closed Guard

identity.objective: Establish collar and sleeve grips from standing, pull guard safely, arrive in closed guard with grips intact.
identity.success: Controlled guard pull. Closed guard established. Grips maintained throughout.
identity.failure: Grips stripped — re-grip (safe). Guard pull sloppy — land in open guard (safe, familiar position).

constraint.task: From standing, get your partner into your closed guard. 90 seconds. No grip instruction.
constraint.discovery: Pulling guard without grips = landing on your back with no control. Grips BEFORE the pull.

isolation.task: Establish and hold collar + sleeve grips for 60 seconds. Partner tries to strip them. Do not pull guard.
isolation.discovery: Grip fighting is a separate skill. The pull is easy — winning grips is hard.

tool_add.effect: Deep four-finger cross grip. Elbow pulls down. Controls their posture even while standing.
Same-side sleeve at the wrist. Denies their posting hand and controls distance.
Sit down. Feet on hips. Pull them into your guard using grips. Close guard immediately.

resistance.level_1: Partner stands still. Allows grips and pull.
resistance.level_2: Partner moves at realistic speed. Does not strip grips.
resistance.level_3: Partner actively strips grips. Circles and changes angles.
resistance.level_4: Full resistance. Partner strip-fights, sprawls, attempts to pass on the pull.

specific_sparring.win: Pull guard → immediately attack (link to S1 or S7)

S9.live: Free roll starting standing. Objective: win grips → pull guard → establish closed guard → begin attacking.

tasks: Strip collar grip immediately. Do not let them get deep.
When they pull guard: stay standing, break grips, begin passing.
Do not follow them to the ground with bad posture.
If they sit without grips: pressure pass immediately.

B1.name: Butterfly Hook Sweep to Knee Cut Pass

identity.objective: Off-balance with underhook, hook sweep to top, pass with knee cut to side control.
identity.success: Sweep to top. Clean knee cut pass. Arrive in side control.
identity.failure: Sweep fails — still in butterfly (safe). Pass fails — return to top half guard (recoverable).

constraint.task: From butterfly guard, try to make your partner fall to either side. 2 minutes.
constraint.discovery: Hooks alone don't sweep. You need upper body control and off-balancing first.

isolation.task: Sweep using only hooks and body movement. No grips, no underhook.
isolation.discovery: Loading their weight onto one hook while kicking the other is the engine.

tool_add.effect: Arm under their armpit. Head on their chest. Now you can direct where they fall.
Load their weight forward. Kick one hook to the ceiling. They topple.
From top: crossface, knee slides across thigh, hip drops, arrive side control.

resistance.level_2: Partner moves realistically. Does not resist once off-balanced.
resistance.level_3: Partner fights the underhook. Bases out against sweep.

B1.live: Free roll. Find butterfly → sweep → pass.

tasks: Base wide against butterfly. Deny the underhook.
When swept: recover guard before they consolidate.
Defend knee cut: inside knee shield, re-pummel.

B2.name: Side Control to Back Take via Nearside Underhook

identity.objective: When opponent turns to escape, recognize back exposure and take the back.
identity.success: Seatbelt → hooks → back control.
identity.failure: Back take missed — still in side control (safe).

constraint.task: From side control, make your partner turn away. 2 minutes. No technique.
constraint.discovery: Crossface pressure makes them turn away — which exposes the back.

isolation.task: Establish seatbelt from side control transition without hooks. Hold 30 seconds.
isolation.discovery: Seatbelt FIRST, then chase hips, then hooks. Never hooks before seatbelt.

tool_add.effect: Drive them to turn away — this is the trigger.
Over-arm and under-arm. Chest glued to back.
Follow their hips. Bottom hook first. Top hook second.

resistance.level_2: Partner turns naturally under pressure.
resistance.level_3: Partner fights the seatbelt, tries to turn back in.

B2.live: Free roll. Objective: find side control → pressure → back take.

tasks: When turning away: tuck elbows, protect neck, prepare to face back in.
Never give seatbelt for free — fight the over-arm.

B3.name: Open Guard Overhead Sweep to Mount

identity.objective: Collar grip control, overhead sweep, arrive in mount.
identity.success: Overhead sweep lands clean. Mount established.
identity.failure: Sweep fails — still in open guard (safe).

constraint.task: From open guard, use only legs to off-balance standing partner. 2 minutes.
constraint.discovery: Feet on hips control distance. Pulling them forward with legs loads the sweep.

isolation.task: Off-balance using only feet and leg hooks.
isolation.discovery: Weight loading onto your feet is the sweep setup. Grips direct the fall.

tool_add.effect: Controls posture and direction of fall.
Feet on hips, pull forward and overhead, kick legs to ceiling, roll them over you.
Follow the sweep immediately. Establish mount before they recover.

resistance.level_2: Partner bases but does not strongly resist.
resistance.level_3: Partner strips grips, bases wide.

B3.live: Free roll. Objective: pull to open guard → overhead sweep → mount.

tasks: Stay heavy. Do not let them load your weight onto their feet.
Strip collar grip early.
When swept: bridge immediately, do not let mount settle.

B4.name: Side Control Americana-to-Armbar Chain

identity.objective: Attack americana. When they defend, transition to armbar.
identity.success: Americana or armbar finish.
identity.failure: Both defended — still in side control (safe).

constraint.task: Maintain side control chest pressure for 3 minutes. No attacks.
constraint.discovery: Heavy side control creates the panic that opens the arms.

isolation.task: Attack americana only. Isolate the near arm. Figure-four. Paint brush.
isolation.discovery: The americana is a lever system. Wrist control + elbow control = submission.

tool_add.effect: Push their wrist to the mat. Control the elbow.
Your wrist on their wrist. Other hand grabs your own wrist. Lever locked.
When they straighten the arm to defend americana: step over head, hips tight, extend.

resistance.level_2: Partner defends weakly.
resistance.level_3: Partner defends americana specifically — creates armbar opening.

B4.live: Free roll. Objective: side control → americana → armbar chain.

tasks: Keep elbows tight. Do not let arm get isolated.
If americana is locked: do NOT straighten arm. Turn into them instead.
Frame and escape before they settle.

B5.name: Closed Guard Kimura Sweep to Top Kimura

identity.objective: Kimura grip from guard. If they resist: sweep. Finish kimura from top.
identity.success: Kimura finish from top or sweep to mount.
identity.failure: Kimura grip lost — still in closed guard (safe).

constraint.task: From closed guard, control one of their arms with both hands. 2 minutes.
constraint.discovery: Two-on-one control is the foundation. The grip IS the position.

isolation.task: Lock the kimura grip (figure-four on wrist). Hold for 30 seconds. Do not sweep or submit.
isolation.discovery: The kimura grip is a control position, not just a submission.

tool_add.effect: Figure-four on their wrist. Elbow tight. Their arm is now your lever.
Open guard, hip out, use kimura grip to rotate them over. Arrive on top.
From top: keep grip, step over head, crank wrist behind their back.

resistance.level_2: Partner resists sweep lightly.
resistance.level_3: Partner fights the grip, bases out.

B5.live: Free roll. Objective: find closed guard → kimura grip → sweep → finish.

tasks: Grip their own wrist/belt to prevent kimura rotation.
Keep elbow glued to body. Do not let arm get extended.
Base wide when they open guard for sweep.

B6.name: Turtle Breakdown to Back Control

identity.objective: Break down turtle position, establish seatbelt, insert hooks, control the back.
identity.success: Turtle broken down. Back control with hooks.
identity.failure: Turtle maintained — still behind them (recoverable). They sit to guard — you're in their guard (safe).

constraint.task: Your partner is in turtle. Get to their back. 2 minutes. No technique instruction.
constraint.discovery: They are protecting their neck and limbs. You need to break their structure before you can take the back.

isolation.task: Establish seatbelt on turtle without hooks. Keep chest-to-back.
isolation.discovery: Seatbelt is the anchor. Without it, you slide off.

tool_add.effect: Over-arm across chest. Under-arm from armpit. Chest glued to their back.
Pull their hips to one side. They collapse off their base.
Bottom hook first (they're sitting on it). Top hook second. Heels inward.

resistance.level_1: Partner stays in turtle. Allows breakdown.
resistance.level_2: Partner turtles actively but does not counter.
resistance.level_3: Partner fights seatbelt, re-turtles, attempts sit-out.

B6.live: Free roll. Objective: when opponent turtles, break down → back control.

tasks: Protect neck. Elbows tight.
Deny the seatbelt — fight the over-arm immediately.
Sit out to guard before they insert hooks.
If hooks are in: fight seatbelt, clear bottom hook, turn to face.

B7.name: De La Riva Sweep to Leg Drag Pass

identity.objective: Establish DLR hook, off-balance, sweep, pass with leg drag.
identity.success: DLR sweep to top. Leg drag pass to side control.
identity.failure: Sweep fails — still in DLR (safe). Hook stripped — re-establish.

constraint.task: Hook one leg with your outside foot from open guard. Keep them off balance. 2 minutes.
constraint.discovery: The DLR hook controls their base on one side. Combined with grip = sweep.

isolation.task: Maintain DLR hook only. Control their balance with just the hook.
isolation.discovery: The hook alone creates an off-balance threat. Grips determine the direction.

tool_add.effect: Outside foot wraps inside their lead leg. Shin against their thigh.
Collar grip pulls down. Ankle grip lifts their far leg. They have nowhere to step.
From top: grab both legs, push them to one side, drive chest across.

resistance.level_2: Partner moves but does not strip hook.
resistance.level_3: Partner strips hook, backs out, re-engages.

specific_sparring.reset: On guard pass or hook strip

B7.live: Free roll. Objective: find DLR → sweep → leg drag → side control.

tasks: Strip the DLR hook immediately — push knee to mat.
Do not let them grab the far ankle.
When swept: recover guard before they initiate the pass.

B8.name: Mount Escape Elbow-Knee to Half Guard Sweep

identity.objective: Escape mount via elbow-knee, arrive in half guard, sweep from bottom half.
identity.success: Mount escaped. Half guard sweep to top.
identity.failure: Escape fails — still under mount (recoverable). Half guard sweep fails — still in half guard (safe).

constraint.task: Survive mount 2 minutes. Find any way to not be flat on your back.
constraint.discovery: Getting to your side and making frames is survival. Flat = dead.

isolation.task: Escape mount using only hip movement and knee insertion. No frames.
isolation.discovery: The knee insertion is the escape. Frames create the space for it.

tool_add.effect: Elbow to inside of knee. Creates a wedge that blocks re-mount.
Trap one leg with yours. You are now in half guard bottom.
Underhook, get to your side, drive into them. Come to top.

resistance.level_2: Top partner maintains actively but does not re-mount.
resistance.level_3: Top partner re-mounts, adjusts, blocks escapes.

specific_sparring.win: Escape → half guard → sweep to top

B8.live: Free roll. When mounted: escape → half guard → sweep.

tasks: When they frame: swim the arm, deny the space.
When knee comes in: sprawl hip to crush the insertion.
In top half guard: crossface, deny the underhook, re-pass.

N1.name: Butterfly Guard Decision Tree

identity.objective: Read opponent's reaction. Branch: hook sweep / arm drag / guillotine based on their defense.
identity.success: Correct read → correct branch → sweep or submission.
identity.failure: Wrong read — return to butterfly (safe). Decision tree resets.

constraint.task: From butterfly, partner chooses one of three reactions. You must recognize which one. No attacks. Just read.
constraint.discovery: Opponent reading is a skill. The attack choice comes FROM the reaction, not before it.

isolation.task: Read and sweep only. No finishes. Focus on reading + correct sweep selection.
isolation.discovery: The decision tree starts with reading, not attacking.

tool_add.effect: Underhook + hook sweep. They are tall = easy to topple.
Arm drag → back take. Their weight is past center.

tool_add.tool: If they reach for your head
tool_add.effect: Guillotine. Chin strap, hip out, squeeze.

resistance.level_2: Partner gives realistic reactions.
resistance.level_3: Partner varies reactions. Mixes timing.

specific_sparring.win: Execute correct branch based on reaction
specific_sparring.reset: On guard pass or wrong read

N1.live: Free roll. Objective: find butterfly → read → execute correct branch.

tasks: Vary your reactions. Do not be predictable.
When you posture: base wide immediately.
When you lean forward: pull head back, deny the drag.

N2.name: Mount Attack System

identity.objective: Decision tree: cross choke → if neck defended → americana → if arm defended → armbar.
identity.success: Finish via whichever branch opens.
identity.failure: All defended — still in mount (safe). Decision tree cycles.

constraint.task: Maintain mount. Partner defends three different ways across rounds. Identify what they're defending.
constraint.discovery: They cannot defend everything. When they protect the neck, arms open. When they protect arms, neck opens.

isolation.task: Cycle only between choke and americana. Read which is available.
isolation.discovery: The two attacks create a dilemma. Add the third later.

tool_add.effect: Arms are pushing up. Isolate near arm → americana.
Neck is exposed. Deep collar grip → cross choke.
Post, ride, when they settle → armbar the posting arm.

resistance.level_2: Partner alternates defenses.
resistance.level_3: Partner defends dynamically + escapes.

N2.live: Free roll. Find mount → cycle the attack system.

tasks: Defend systematically: neck first, then arms, then escape.
Never defend only one thing — you will lose the other.
Escape attempts should be timed between attack transitions.

N3.name: Back Attack System

identity.objective: Decision tree: RNC → if chin defended → armbar → if arm defended → collar choke (gi).
identity.success: Finish via the open branch.
identity.failure: All defended — maintain back control (safe). Re-cycle.

constraint.task: Hold back control for 3 minutes. Partner escapes actively. Maintain position only.
constraint.discovery: Back retention is the prerequisite. Without retention, no attack system works.

isolation.task: Hunt RNC only. When chin is defended, just re-adjust. Do not give up position.
isolation.discovery: The hand fight IS the back attack. Most of the work is hand fighting, not choking.

tool_add.effect: Switch to armbar — they pull arms down to defend neck, arm is exposed.
Hand fight → slide choking arm under chin → RNC.
Let them — you land in mount. Still dominant.

resistance.level_2: Partner hand fights realistically.
resistance.level_3: Partner escapes actively + defends dynamically.

N3.live: Free roll. Take back → cycle the attack system.

tasks: Two hands on the choking arm at all times.
Clear bottom hook first — this is your escape route.
Turn into them, not away. Turning away = worse back control.

N4.name: Half Guard Passing Network

identity.objective: Decision tree: knee slice → if they re-guard → crossface smash pass → if underhook → backstep.
identity.success: Pass to side control via correct branch.
identity.failure: Pass fails — still in half guard (recoverable). They re-guard — restart.

constraint.task: From top half guard, free your leg. 2 minutes. Any method.
constraint.discovery: Every pass attempt creates a reaction. The reaction tells you which pass is next.

isolation.task: Cycle only between knee slice and smash pass. Read which is available.
isolation.discovery: Two passes create a binary decision. Add the third later.

tool_add.effect: Knee slice. Shin across thigh. Hip drops. Clean pass.

tool_add.tool: If they get to their side
tool_add.effect: Crossface heavy. Smash pass — drive them flat, walk around.
Backstep. Spin to the other side. They lose the underhook.

resistance.level_2: Partner holds actively, one defense pattern.
resistance.level_3: Partner switches defenses.
resistance.level_4: Full half guard game from bottom.

specific_sparring.win: Pass to SCTR.CTRL via any branch

N4.live: Free roll. Find top half guard → read → correct pass.

tasks: Get to your side immediately. Do not stay flat.
Win the underhook. Head position follows the underhook.
If they knee slice: block with knee shield. If they smash: re-pummel underhook.

N5.name: Open Guard Retention System

identity.objective: Against passing pressure: retain guard → re-guard → sweep or submit when they overcommit.
identity.success: Guard retained. Counter-sweep or submission when passer overcommits.
identity.failure: Guard passed — recover from side control (S6 territory).

constraint.task: Your partner passes for 3 minutes. Your only job: do not let them achieve side control.
constraint.discovery: Guard retention is not one technique — it's continuous adjustment. Frames, knees, hips.

isolation.task: Retain guard only. No attacking. Just survive and re-guard for 3 minutes.
isolation.discovery: Retention without attacking is the foundation. Counter-attacks come after retention is automatic.

tool_add.effect: Creates distance. Denies chest-to-chest contact.
Knees track their hips. If their hips move, your knees follow.
When they clear one leg: immediately re-insert the other. Never let both legs be cleared.

resistance.level_2: Partner passes at realistic speed, one direction.
resistance.level_3: Partner chain-passes, switches sides.

specific_sparring.win: Retain guard for 3 minutes OR counter-sweep
specific_sparring.reset: On clean pass to side control

N5.live: Free roll. Objective: when in open guard, retain. Sweep or submit only when the opening is clear.

tasks: Chain passes — do not attack the same side twice.
Clear frames before advancing.
When they re-pummel: pin the re-inserted leg before it re-establishes.

N6.name: De La Riva / RDLR Guard System

identity.objective: Flow between DLR and RDLR based on opponent's passing direction. Sweep or back take from either.
identity.success: Sweep to top or back take.
identity.failure: Guard passed — recover (recoverable). Hook stripped — re-establish (safe).

constraint.task: Maintain one hook (DLR or RDLR) for 2 minutes while partner tries to pass.
constraint.discovery: DLR and RDLR are two sides of the same position. They pass one, you switch to the other.

isolation.task: Sweep only. No back takes. Focus on off-balance + hook selection.
isolation.discovery: The sweep threat opens the back take. Without sweep threat, they ignore your hooks.

tool_add.effect: When they step to pass DLR: switch hook to RDLR on the other leg.
Invert slightly, lift their base leg, they sit down.
Collar grip + DLR hook + inversion → spin under → take the back.

resistance.level_1: Partner stays still in your guard.
resistance.level_2: Partner passes one direction only.
resistance.level_3: Partner changes directions, strips hooks.

N6.live: Free roll. Objective: find DLR/RDLR → flow between them → sweep or back take.

tasks: Strip hooks aggressively.
Do not stand in one place — movement kills DLR.
When they invert: pressure forward immediately.

N7.name: Side Control Submission Network

identity.objective: Chain attacks: americana → kimura → arm triangle → north-south choke based on defensive reactions.
identity.success: Finish via whatever the opponent leaves open.
identity.failure: All defended — still in side control (safe). Chain resets.

constraint.task: Maintain side control for 3 minutes against active escapes. No attacks.
constraint.discovery: Side control maintenance under pressure is the prerequisite for all attacks.

isolation.task: Cycle americana → kimura only. Read which arm is available.
isolation.discovery: The two attacks use the same grip family. One flows to the other.

tool_add.effect: Americana. Figure-four. Paint brush.
Kimura on far arm. Same grip, different angle.
Arm triangle. Trap their arm next to their neck. Walk to finish side.
Transition north-south. North-south choke.

resistance.level_2: Partner defends two attacks.
resistance.level_3: Partner defends dynamically + attempts escape.

N7.live: Free roll. Find side control → cycle the attack network.

tasks: Keep elbows tight to body.
Do not push on their head — it opens the arm triangle.
Frame and escape early. Once they start cycling, defense gets harder.
